results.possibleIdentity: Moses Sorkin was an engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, associated with TRW Systems in Santa Monica and Redondo Beach, California. He was a member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) organization, where he was listed in their technical services directory as a specialist in electrical engineering, acoustics, sound generation, sonar, ultrasonics, audio systems, audio-visual conversions, and instrumentation and control systems. This range of expertise aligns with TRW's historical focus on underwater defense systems and aerospace technology. In his later career, a Moses Sorkin appears as a researcher and co-author on scientific papers related to fuel cell technology (specifically direct methanol fuel cells) published between 2006 and 2008 in the Journal of The Electrochemical Society. In these publications, he collaborated with prominent electrochemical engineers such as Arthur Kaufman and H. Frank Gibbard, both of whom held leadership roles at Giner, Inc. and had previous associations with large engineering firms like Gould Inc., which also operated in the sonar and battery sectors.
